summary: This notebook demonstrates how to fine-tune the Gemma 2 2B causal language model using Keras 3 and parameter-efficient LoRA on a domain-specific dataset of Bhagavad Gita verses. It walks through loading a reduced 52-row CSV, formatting the text into a structured prompt template, and compiling the model with sparse categorical crossentropy for training. The author then evaluates the fine-tuned model on both in-distribution verses and out-of-distribution Hindi questions to assess its generative capabilities. Readers should learn how to adapt Keras 3's keras-nlp API for efficient LLM fine-tuning and manage GPU memory constraints on Kaggle.
competition_flow: Loads a small CSV dataset of Bhagavad Gita verses, formats them into prompts, fine-tunes a Gemma 2 2B model with LoRA using Keras 3, and evaluates the output on in-distribution and out-of-distribution queries.
data_processing: Custom prompt template formatting using df.apply to map Verse, WordMeaning, and HinMeaning columns into a structured string
Reduced dataset size to 52 rows to manage GPU memory
insights: LoRA with a low rank (4) enables efficient fine-tuning of a 2B parameter model on limited GPU memory.
Reducing the training dataset to 52 rows is a practical workaround for Kaggle's GPU quota constraints.
The model can generate plausible answers for out-of-distribution questions even without explicit training on them, demonstrating some zero-shot generalization capability.
